name: Rose Levy Beranbaum's Best Bittersweet Brownies
recipeIngredient: 4 ounces European bittersweet chocolate, finely chopped
1 stick unsalted butter (8 tablespoons), cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees and position a rack in the center.
2. Line an 8-inch baking pan with wax paper.
3. Lightly butter and flour the inside of the pan, tapping out excess flour.
4. In the top part of a double boiler over hot (not simmering) water, melt chocolate and butter, stirring often until smooth.
5. Remove from heat and let stand for 10 minutes until tepid.
6. Whisk in sugar. Add eggs one at a time, then vanilla.
7. Using a wooden spoon, stir in flour and salt just until blended.
8. Stir in pecans.
9. Spread batter evenly into the prepared pan.
10. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes until a toothpick inserted 1 inch from the side comes out with a moist crumb (the center will appear wet).
11. Cool on a wire rack for 20 minutes.
12. Run a knife around the inside edge of the pan, invert onto a lightly buttered baking sheet, and cool completely.
13. Cut into 16 squares.
14. Wrap each brownie tightly in plastic wrap.
